title: Love & Attachment Theory

analysis.object: T241: Love and attachment theory depth analysis

cortisol_stress.description: Cortisol in heartbreak: prolonged stress response

grief_oscillation.description: Grief cycles: waves of acute grief and adjustment

infatuation.description: Early romantic love: oscillatory intrusive thoughts
infatuation.reason: Rumination = oscillatory EML-3 (same as OCD intrusive thoughts)

deep_attachment.reason: Stable measurement: partner = reliable log-scale reference

love_as_two_become_one.description: Becoming more than the sum of two individuals
love_as_two_become_one.reason: Categorification: emergent whole beyond finite description

analysis.depth_transition_question: Is falling in love a depth transition? Answer: YES — it is Δd = -1. Infatuation: EML-3 (oscillatory intrusion, rumination). Deep attachment: EML-2 (stable logarithmic base, secure measurement). Falling in love = EML-3 → EML-2 transition (depth reduction by 1). Breakup/loss reverses it: EML-2 → EML-3 (grief oscillation returns). The depth change Δd=-1 is the transition from passion to love.

analysis.verdict: Infatuation: EML-3. Deep love: EML-2. Falling in love = Δd=-1 transition.
analysis.theorem: T241: Love Depth — infatuation EML-3, attachment EML-2; love = Δd=-1

key_theorem: T241: Love Depth (S520). Infatuation: EML-3 (oscillatory rumination). Deep attachment: EML-2 (stable logarithmic base). Falling in love = Δd=-1: oscillation settles into measurement. Heartbreak reverses it: EML-2 → EML-3 (grief oscillation). Two becoming one: EML-∞ (emergent beyond finite description).
rabbit_hole_log: Deep attachment: partner as stable reference → EML-2
T241: Love = depth reduction; loss = depth increase
